prompt: My ESP32 firmware keeps crashing after about 6 hours of uptime. It's a sensor hub that reads from 4 I2C sensors every 100ms and sends data over MQTT. I'm using Arduino framework with C++. Each sensor read does a `new SensorReading()` and the MQTT publish does `new String(json)`. Memory seems fine at startup but free heap drops over time. What's going on and how do I fix it?
assertions: Correctly identifies heap fragmentation as the root cause of the crash-after-hours pattern
Refactored code replaces new/delete with static allocation, pools, or pre-allocated buffers
Replaces Arduino String class with fixed-size char buffers (char[], snprintf)
Suggests heap monitoring (ESP.getFreeHeap, ESP.getMaxAllocHeap, or equivalent)
Includes concrete refactored code, not just advice
